source_jobs.title: Workplace Experience Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.



Overview:

We’re looking for a Workplace Experience Coordinator who brings ownership, judgment, and pride to the details that shape how our employees, candidates, vendors, and visitors experience the company.

This is a highly visible, hands-on role at the center of our office operations. You’ll help keep the workplace running smoothly, create moments that make people feel supported and welcomed, and become one of the go-to people employees trust to get things done.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone early in their career who has the instincts of a future high performer: highly organized, proactive, resourceful, energized by people, and motivated by making every detail better than it has to be.



What you'll do:

 - Daily office operations and facility readiness: Ensuring the physical workspace is clean, stocked, and functional throughout the day, responding to real-time employee needs, and maintaining a consistent standard of environment across all common areas

 - Vendor and facilities oversight: Acting as the primary liaison between the office and external service providers, proactively managing supply levels, and escalating building or maintenance issues to resolution

 - Guest and candidate experience management: Serving as the first point of contact for all visitors, creating a professional and welcoming impression that reflects the company's culture and standards

 - Administrative reporting and budget management: Owning expense processing, budget tracking, and recurring reporting to leadership, maintaining visibility into office spend and operational performance



Who you are

 - Exceptionally organized: you can manage multiple competing priorities without letting things slip through the cracks.

 - Proactive and ownership-driven: you see what needs to be done, take initiative, and follow through without waiting to be asked.

 - Resourceful and solutions-oriented: you stay calm when things are ambiguous, solve problems creatively, and find a path forward.

 - A strong communicator: you keep the right people informed, communicate clearly, and know when to ask questions or escalate.

 - Detail-oriented with high standards: you notice what others miss, take pride in getting things right, and care about the quality of every detail.

 - Warm and professional: you represent Credit Genie well to every employee, candidate, vendor, and visitor who walks through the door.

 - Comfortable with technology: you can use tools like Google Workspace, Slack, Notion, and basic office systems, and you learn new systems quickly.

 - Discreet and trustworthy: you handle confidential employee and company information with sound judgment and care.

 - High-potential and eager to grow: you may be early in your career, but you bring curiosity, humility, strong judgment, and a desire to build something excellent.



Requirements

 - Ownership mentality: Takes responsibility for outcomes, not just assigned tasks. Notices what needs to be done, follows through without being chased, and treats the office experience as something they personally own.

 - High agency and resourcefulness: Finds ways to sol

source_jobs.title: Staff iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW

We are looking for a Staff iOS Engineer to help architect our Mobile Apps for the next 2-3 years of growth. The ideal candidate should have experience leading the design and development of mobile apps with high quality, stability, and low latency.

Are you passionate about building high-quality iOS apps and excited by the challenge of adding new products and features? As a Staff iOS Engineer, you'll lead key components of our mobile app as we expand into brand new product areas. You'll collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to ensure our mobile app scales and evolves to meet the needs of millions of users.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Collaborate with designers, product managers, and engineers to architect new products and define a compelling UX that functions well within an iOS mobile app

 - Provide iOS mobile app expertise, helping improve the team's development practices, design patterns, deployment processes, and customer experience

 - Work with stakeholders from product, marketing, etc. on initiatives aligning with our KPIs and business priorities

 - Write code for app design, features, components, and integrations in a clean, readable, and reusable fashion

 - Contribute to all phases of the development lifecycle

 - Participate in standard engineering practices such as code reviews, stand-ups, planning meetings, and product/technology demos

 - Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, evaluating their potential to enhance the Credit Genie product suite and maintain our competitive edge

 - Mentor and develop other engineers on the team
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 8+ years of professional mobile development

 - Proficient in Swift (We use Swift 6)

 - Deep understanding of mobile app UX best practices

 - Experience leading large-scale projects and partnering directly with product, marketing, and engineering teams in a highly iterative environment

 - Ability to propose, refine, implement, and update UX/product solutions to ensure they meet customer needs

 - Proficiency in taking design mocks (including Figma files) and coding them in-app using Swift

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Excellent communication skills
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working on personal finance management (PFM) products.

 - Understanding of UX design concepts (e.g. density, diversity) and the languages, systems, principles, and processes used in design creation

 - Comfort using design prototyping tools (e.g. Figma, Invision, Origami, or Sketch)

 - Ability to maintain a style guide, SwiftUI, and brand

 - Familiarity with consumer credit risk and the customer life cycle


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to provide a comprehensive offering of benefits and perks that promote better financial, mental, and physical wellness.



We believe working alongside each other in person is the best way to build a great product and foster a strong company culture. Our expectation is that employees are in the office five days a week, allowing for optimal collaboration, inclusivity, and productivity. At the same time, we understand that life happens and recognize the impo

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Trust Platform
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW

As a Senior Backend Engineer on the Trust Platform team, you'll play a pivotal role in shaping the systems that safeguard and personalize our financial products. You'll design and build the core services that power fraud detection, authentication, decisioning, and customer data. This is a rare chance to join a fast-growing fintech startup where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on both the company and the financial well-being of our users.

What You'll Do

 - Design, Build, and Scale – Own backend systems end-to-end, from architecture to deployment, building high-performance, secure, and reusable services that product teams rely on to manage risk and deliver consistent customer experiences.

 - Greenfield Development – Work on new capabilities from scratch - whether that's a next-generation fraud signal pipeline, a unified notifications service, or an intelligent decisioning layer - making foundational technical decisions that will shape our platform for years to come.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration – Partner with product, design, data science, and adjacent engineering teams to build trust and safety features that balance rigorous risk management with a seamless user experience.

 - Technical Leadership – Lead critical technical and design discussions across fraud detection, authentication, and customer data domains, driving best practices in system design, coding, testing, and DevOps.

 - Customer-Focused Innovation – Scope, build, and optimize intelligent services that protect users from fraud while ensuring every interaction feels secure, personalized, and trustworthy.




REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years of experience designing, building, and scaling high-performance backend systems in a fast-paced, high-growth environment. 

 - Expertise in architecting and productionizing large-scale distributed systems.

 - Comfortable working across multiple technical areas and collaborating with frontend/mobile teams.

 - Passion for tackling complex challenges with structured thinking and analytical rigor. Ability to break down intricate systems, identify flaws, and implement scalable, secure, and maintainable solutions.

 - Thrives in a fast-moving, iterative environment with a bias for action. Excited to take full ownership of projects, drive impact, and work cross-functionally with product, design, and engineering teams.

 - Strong understanding of data privacy, compliance, and secure coding practices. Experience with designing resilient and reliable systems that meet high security and regulatory standards.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with products related to lending, credit cards, payments, or other financial services.

 - Familiarity with mobile app ecosystems and financial technology


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to provide a comprehensive offering of benefits and perks that promote better financial, mental, and physical wellness.



We believe working alongside each other in person is the best way to build a great product and foster a strong company culture. Our expectation is that employees are in the office five days a week, allowing for optimal col

source_jobs.title: Technical Product & Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

About the Role

We are looking for a Technical Product & Program Manager who thrives at the intersection of product thinking and execution discipline. This is a role for someone who can define what we build and make sure it actually ships- on time, with the right quality, and with full cross-functional alignment.

You will own the full product development lifecycle for key initiatives: from shaping requirements and writing specs to running agile ceremonies, managing dependencies, and tracking the metrics that tell us whether we are winning. You will work closely with engineering, design, data, and business stakeholders to keep our teams moving fast without losing clarity on what matters most.

If you have built products at a high-growth fintech or payments company, know how to translate ambiguity into actionable tickets, and can hold a room of engineers and designers accountable while keeping the energy collaborative.


What you’ll do

 - Partner with engineering and design to define product requirements, write clear specs, and prioritize features and bugs that move our core metrics.

 - Lead planning and execution across product and engineering initiatives, ensuring goals, timelines, and deliverables are clearly defined and aligned.

 - Drive agile methodologies- facilitate sprint planning, daily stand-ups, backlog refinement, and retrospectives to maintain team velocity and morale.

 - Own ticket and task hygiene: ensure all work is actionable, well-documented, and sized correctly before it enters a sprint.

 - Monitor project health, identify risks early, and communicate status clearly to stakeholders and leadership.

 - Build and maintain dashboards and metrics that give the team and leadership real-time visibility into progress, velocity, and product outcomes.

 - Measure the success of launched features and iterate based on data, user feedback, and business goals.

 - Work closely with design, developers, and QA to ensure delivery meets functional requirements and user expectations.

 - Interface comfortably with technical complexity- understand system architecture, API dependencies, and infrastructure constraints well enough to unblock teams and make smart tradeoffs.


Requirements

 - 5+ years of experience in product management, technical program management, or a closely related role at a technology company.

 - Demonstrated ability to own both the “what” (product definition, requirements, roadmap) and the “how” (execution, sprint management, cross-team coordination).

 - Experience in fintech, payments, or financial services. You understand how payment infrastructure, compliance constraints, and customer trust intersect.

 - Strong command of agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) and the judgment to know when to adapt the process to fit the team.

 - Comfort with technical depth: APIs, mobile app development, data pipelines, and dev ops concepts should feel familiar, not foreign.

 - Exceptional communication skills. You can write a crisp one-pager, run a tight stand-up, and give a clear status update to a C-suite audience.

 - Proficiency with project management and product tooling

source_jobs.title: QA Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

Overview

As the Engineering Manager for our QA team, you’ll lead a talented group of QA engineers responsible for validating and continuously improving the quality of our products across mobile and web. You’ll be accountable for test strategy, automation, and release readiness across the stack, partnering closely with Engineering, Product, Design, and Data to embed quality best practices throughout the SDLC and ship reliable, trustworthy customer-facing experiences.

This is a rare opportunity to join a fast-growing fintech startup where your work will have a direct, meaningful impact on both the company and the financial well-being of our users.



What You'll Do

 - Lead and grow a team of QA engineers, providing mentorship, coaching, and career development support to help individuals thrive and advance in their roles

 - Define and drive the quality roadmap in partnership with Engineering, Product, Design, and Data leaders; aligning testing strategy, automation, and release readiness with business priorities

 - Scale and improve automation frameworks and regression/smoke/integration suites to increase coverage, keep developer feedback loops fast, and improve deployment speed and confidence

 - Embed testing into CI/CD to reduce manual effort and prevent regressions, and drive release readiness and incident learning to avoid recurring issues

 - Promote operational excellence across the SDLC by setting quality best practices for test planning, exploratory testing, defect triage, and metrics; focused on defect prevention, observable quality signals, and continuous improvement

 - Ensure testing covers performance, security, and regulatory/compliance needs for fintech products

 - Hire and onboard QA engineers, helping scale a diverse, high-performing team while maintaining a strong bar for talent and culture

 - Manage and continuously improve team processes, partnering with engineering teams on planning, execution, and retrospectives to drive effective collaboration and predictable delivery
   
   

Requirements

 - 3+ years of experience managing SDET/QA/Quality Engineering teams of 5+

 - 5+ years of hands-on experience in software testing and test automation for production systems across mobile and web

 - Experience building and operating testing programs for mission-critical products at scale, with a strong focus on automation, release readiness, reliability, performance testing, and regression prevention

 - Proven track record of leading teams to deliver complex, cross-functional initiatives and improving quality outcomes

 - Strong cross-functional communication skills, able to influence engineering and product partners effectively

 - Experienced in mentoring future leaders and growing talent

 - Fosters a culture of ownership, accountability, and urgency

 - Familiar with incident management, release readiness, and on-call/production support collaboration

 - Comfortable working in fast-paced, iterative environments with a bias for action

 - Solid understanding of security, data privacy, and regulatory/compliance requirements in fintech


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to pro

source_jobs.title: Director of Analytics
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

OVERVIEW

Join our Analytics team to drive deeper understanding of customer behavior and product performance across our financial wellness platform. As Director of Analytics, you will lead the analytics strategy that informs product decisions, growth, and customer outcomes.

You will partner with Product, Marketing, Risk, Engineering, and User Research to define success metrics, uncover insights, and embed data-driven decision making across the product lifecycle while leading a team of product analysts.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Define and lead company-wide analytics strategy, including measurement frameworks, reporting standards, experimentation practices, and decision intelligence capabilities.

 - Build and mentor a high-performing team of analysts responsible for delivering insights that shape product strategy and roadmap decisions.

 - Define and monitor core product KPIs including activation, engagement, retention, funnel performance, and lifetime value.

 - Lead deep-dive analyses of user behavior, feature adoption, and customer journeys to uncover growth opportunities and inform product strategy.

 - Establish and scale experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing and experimentation design, to evaluate product features and initiatives.

 - Partner with internal teams to shape product roadmaps and prioritize initiatives based on measurable customer and business impact.

 - Ensure rigorous measurement of new features and product initiatives to validate impact and guide iteration.

 - Build dashboards and self-serve analytics that enable stakeholders to monitor product performance and make informed decisions.

 - Partner with Data Engineering to strengthen metric definitions, data models, semantic layer adoption, and governed reporting.

REQUIREMENTS

 - 8–10+ years of experience in product analytics, data science, or quantitative analytics, ideally in fintech or consumer tech.

 - 3–5+ years of experience leading analytics teams.

 - Master’s degree in Analytics, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or similar quantitative field.

 - Advanced SQL and strong Python or similar analytical programming skills.

 - Experience with BI tools such as Sigma, Tableau, Looker, or similar platforms.

 - Strong experience with product metrics, experimentation, and growth analytics.

 - Familiarity with modern data platforms such as AWS, Snowflake, and dbt.

 - Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills.

 - Comfortable operating in fast-paced, high-growth environments.


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to provide a comprehensive offering of benefits and perks that promote better financial, mental, and physical wellness.



We believe working alongside each other in person is the best way to build a great product and foster a strong company culture. Our expectation is that employees are in the office five days a week, allowing for optimal collaboration, inclusivity, and productivity. At the same time, we understand that life happens and recognize the importance of flexibility. We are committed to supporting our employees when circumstances arise that require re

source_jobs.title: Payment Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW

Credit Genie helps people get cash when they need it and build toward financial stability. Every advance and every repayment is a money-movement event across ACH and card rails, and as we scale toward millions of transactions, payments is the system that decides whether members get paid on time and whether the business is sustainable.

We're hiring our first Payments Product Lead to own that system as a product. This is a net-new function. You will be the single owner of how money moves: the rails, the funding instruments, the orchestration, the reconciliation, and the reliability and cost of all of it. You'll report to the Head of Product and lead through Engineering, Risk, Compliance, and Finance.

This is a senior individual-contributor role for someone who wants to build a function, not inherit one — you'll have the mandate, the executive air cover, and the data to move fast and own the outcome.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Rail and instrument strategy. Decide when we use ACH vs. card, how fallbacks work, and how funding instruments are kept healthy. Put change-control around it so payment logic never ships un-designed.

 - Funding-instrument health. Own the card-update, AVS, and re-instrument flows that ensure funding instruments remain healthy

 - Payment orchestration. Own cadence, retries, and the prevention of stuck or orphaned transactions at the system level.

 - Reconciliation and settlement. Own ledger integrity, settlement timing, and the money-movement path across our systems.

 - Reliability and cost as outcomes. Own the payments scorecard (approval rate, outcomes, cost per dollar moved, money-movement uptime) and drive Engineering to hit it.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 8+ years in payments product, including meaningful time owning strategy and roadmap end to end.

 - Deep ACH and card-network expertise: return/response codes, retry and representment rules, AVS/BIN behavior, settlement timing. You can explain why a specific instrument fails and design around it.

 - System-level fluency in rail orchestration, fallback design, reconciliation, and ledgering.

 - You leverage AI and diagnose from data. You define the metric; you don't wait for a dashboard.

 - A track record shipping on high-volume payment systems where you owned outcomes (rates, cost, reliability), not just features.

What makes someone exceptional here

You've productized payments operations before. You've built or driven the tooling and automation that turns payment diagnosis into a standing capability, and you treat AI and agents as a way to build that platform, not as a buzzword. You move cross-functional teams through clarity and credibility rather than authority, and you take the reliability and cost numbers personally.

What this role is not

This is a product role. You are accountable for payments reliability and cost as outcomes and you design the system that delivers them, but on-call and incident command live with our Engineering and SRE teams. If you're looking to run a payments-operations desk or a pager rotation, this isn't it.




PREFERRED

 - EWA or consumer fintech lending experience.

 - Familia

source_jobs.title: Senior Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

Overview

As a recruiter, you will lead recruitment efforts to identify uniquely accomplished individuals to guide us forward. You’ll serve as a consultative partner to our hiring managers and leadership team, owning the full recruiting lifecycle across every corner of the business. One quarter you might be filling engineering roles; the next, finance, product, or operations. Breadth and adaptability are requirements, not bonuses.

What you’ll do

 - Serve as a consultative advisor to hiring managers and leadership across engineering, product, finance, G&A, and beyond. Leading strategic hiring discussions and building teams holistically

 - Manage the full-cycle recruitment process across functions, ensuring a smooth and positive candidate experience from first outreach through offer close

 - Partner with the broader People team to build efficient and scalable recruiting processes, interview loop design, structured evaluation frameworks, offer workflows, and sourcing playbooks

 - Leverage data to evaluate and optimize current hiring processes for operational efficiency; bring pipeline metrics and market insights to hiring partners to influence decisions and set realistic expectations

 - Source active and passive candidates through a variety of channels — online databases, communities, events, employee referrals, and creative approaches that go beyond the obvious

 - Leverage AI tools to drive efficiency across sourcing, outreach personalization, candidate pipeline analysis, and market research — staying ahead of how the recruiting function is evolving

 - Champion diverse and inclusive hiring pipelines by building proactive sourcing strategies that reach candidates from underrepresented communities, because you understand why it matters

 - Manage mission-critical stakeholder relationships within the business, providing appropriate context and perspective while working toward aligned, accountable hiring outcomes

Who you are

 - 8+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience at an innovative, scaling organization, with a proven track record of closing strong candidates in competitive markets

 - Ability to recruit across multiple functions - engineering, product, finance, and business roles. A deep technical recruiting background is not required, but range and the ability to ramp quickly on any role type is

 - Ability to build and implement recruiting strategies from the ground up in a 0-to-1 environment, creating structure and process where none previously existed

 - Ability to thrive in a data and metrics-driven environment. You measure success holistically, think beyond time-to-fill, and use data to influence hiring partners and drive process improvement

 - Consultative communication skills when working with stakeholders at all levels of the organization. You set expectations clearly, push back constructively when needed, and earn trust through transparency

 - Ability to navigate ambiguity and adapt when priorities shift. You recalibrate quickly, hold yourself accountable, and bring a steady, solutions-oriented approach to changing conditions

 - You leverage AI tools in your day-to-

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

Credit Genie is looking for its first Product Marketing hire, a strategic and hands-on leader who will build our product marketing function from the ground up. This is a rare opportunity to define our GTM strategy, own product positioning across the portfolio, and shape how our products and brand come to life through clear, differentiated messaging that drives adoption and growth.

We’re looking for someone who can think big and execute fast. You’ll be equally comfortable developing high-level strategy and rolling up your sleeves to write, test, and launch. If you’re energized by building from 0 to 1, collaborating cross-functionally, and driving measurable impact, this role is for you.

You’ll work closely with Product, Design, Growth Marketing, Research, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Brand to ensure our products are clearly positioned, successfully launched, and deeply understood by customers.

What you’ll do:

 - Lead GTM strategy: Develop & execute the strategy for our new products and features, ensuring alignment with overall business objectives and customer needs. Lead cross functional planning ensuring impactful internal and external communications.

 - Own product positioning and messaging: Craft clear, compelling product narrative and value propositions that ladder up to our broader brand strategy. Ensure product positioning reinforces brand promise, differentiation, and long-term category leadership. Build scalable frameworks for consistent storytelling across all touchpoints.

 - Own product matrix: Define how Credit Genie’s products and features fit together across the customer journey, including naming, tiering, and value articulation. Ensure clarity, consistency, and differentiation across the full product suite.

 - Drive product growth: Partner closely with product, CRM, and growth marketing teams to develop in-product messaging, onboarding, lifecycle comms, and engagement strategies that improve activation, retention, and expansion.

 - Champion market & customer insights: Collaborate with Research and Data Science to turn user insights and market trends into actionable strategies that shape product direction and marketing execution.

 - Establish product marketing operating rigor: Build repeatable GTM processes, playbooks, and launch frameworks that scale as the company grows. Define success metrics, post-launch evaluation, and iteration plans to continuously improve performance.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Navigate complex stakeholder environments and communicate effectively with Product, Design, Compliance, and Customer Service to ensure cohesive GTM alignment.

Requirements:

 - 7+ years of experience leading all aspects of Product Marketing in a B2C environment

 - Proven track record of leading GTM for consumer products (0-1) under stringent regulatory and quality requirements.

 - Experience defining product positioning, messaging frameworks, or portfolio-level narratives across multiple products.

 - Strong blend of strategic thinking and hands-on execution. You can write, analyze, and execute as effectively as you can plan.

 - Excellent communicat

source_jobs.title: Senior User Researcher
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW


At Credit Genie, we build financial tools that improve people's lives — and research ensures we're solving the right problems. We're looking for a Senior User Researcher to lead how we understand our customers, their needs, and opportunities to improve. You'll own the full research lifecycle, from discovery to delivery, using a mix of qualitative and quantitative methods. This role requires someone who combines strong research rigor with creativity, moves quickly, and enjoys engaging directly with customers.

What you'll do

 - Own research strategy and roadmap for key product areas, prioritizing based on impact and risk.

 - Design and execute end-to-end studies, from scoping through synthesis and activation.

 - Conduct customer interviews, usability testing, surveys, and feedback sessions to uncover insights.

 - Blend qualitative and quantitative data to develop clear, actionable recommendations.

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product, Design, Engineering, and Data to influence decisions.

 - Translate research into compelling insights, frameworks, and artifacts that shape product direction.

 - Advocate for the customer in roadmap and strategy discussions.

 - Contribute to research operations, best practices, and mentorship.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 5–8+ years of UX/User Research experience in product environments.

 - Strong mixed-methods expertise (qualitative and quantitative).

 - Proven ability to lead customer interviews and research initiatives end-to-end.

 - Excellent study design, analytical thinking, and communication skills.

 - Experience with common research and analytics tools.

 - High ownership, strong judgment, and ability to operate in ambiguity.

 - Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Fintech or regulated industry experience.

 - Experience with advanced research methods (e.g., segmentation, experimentation).

 - Background in building or scaling research practices.


SUCCESS IN THIS ROLE

 - Research meaningfully influences product strategy and decisions.

 - Strong, ongoing feedback loops with customers.

 - Faster, more confident decision-making across teams.

 - High engagement from cross-functional partners.


GROWTH OPPORTUNITIES

 - Expand research across multiple product areas.

 - Build and lead a research team.

 - Shape Credit Genie's research practice and culture.


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to provide a comprehensive offering of benefits and perks that promote better financial, mental, and physical wellness.



We believe working alongside each other in person is the best way to build a great product and foster a strong company culture. Our expectation is that employees are in the office five days a week, allowing for optimal collaboration, inclusivity, and productivity. At the same time, we understand that life happens and recognize the importance of flexibility. We are committed to supporting our employees when circumstances arise that require remote work or adjusted schedules. Our goal is to ensure everyone can effectively balance personal and professional responsibilities while maintain

source_jobs.title: Chief Compliance Officer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW

The Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) is responsible for designing, implementing, and leading a compliance program that enables innovation while ensuring regulatory and legal adherence. The CCO ensures that compliance is integrated into business strategy, allowing the company to develop customer-centric, compliant financial solutions without unnecessary friction.

The CCO must maintain the effectiveness of the compliance program, ensuring it adapts to regulatory developments, business growth, and product expansion. They are responsible for providing senior management and the Board of Directors with transparency into the company’s compliance posture, offering clear reporting on program efficacy, risk trends, and regulatory challenges.

Beyond maintaining compliance, the CCO is a business enabler, partnering with product, engineering, design and operations teams to develop solutions that meet both regulatory requirements and customer needs. This requires balancing risk management with a practical, scalable approach that fosters innovation while maintaining regulatory integrity.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Develops a compliance framework that allows the company to build and scale innovative financial products within legal and regulatory guidelines.

 - Advises leadership on how compliance can be a differentiator in the Earned Wage Access market.

 - Tracks regulatory developments and proactively adapts compliance programs to stay ahead of potential challenges.

 - Engages with regulators, industry groups, and policymakers to help shape the future of EWA regulations.

 - Builds scalable compliance solutions that grow with the company’s expansion into new markets and product lines.

 - Works hand-in-hand with product, engineering and design teams to ensure compliance is embedded into the product design process early, not just as a final approval step.

 - Advises on structuring products to balance compliance with customer experience and market competitiveness.

 - Ensures compliance processes are designed to be lightweight and efficient, reducing operational friction.

 - Guide the management team on compliance strategy, ensuring that regulatory considerations are seamlessly integrated into business decisions and product innovation.

 - Deliver clear, data-driven compliance reports to the Board, providing actionable insights on program effectiveness, regulatory trends, and key risk areas to support strategic decision-making.

 - Develops a compliance-first culture where teams see regulations as guardrails for smart innovation, rather than roadblocks.

 - Educates employees on practical compliance principles rather than overwhelming them with rigid policies.

 - Works cross-functionally with leadership, legal, risk, and finance to align compliance strategies with business objectives.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 10+ years of experience in compliance, legal, or risk management roles within fintech, financial services, or adjacent regulated industries

 - Proven track record of building or scaling compliance programs in product-driven, fast-paced environments, ideally at a Series A–C stage and/or technology

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.


OVERVIEW

As a Staff Software Engineer, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping our platform, designing and building the core systems that power our financial products. You’ll have the opportunity to architect scalable infrastructure, drive technical direction, and work on products from the ground up. This is a rare chance to join a fast-growing fintech startup where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on both the company and the financial well-being of our users.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, Build, and Scale – Own backend systems end-to-end, from architecture to deployment, ensuring high performance, security, and scalability for next-generation financial products.

 - Greenfield Development – Work on new projects from scratch, making foundational technical decisions that will shape our platform for years to come.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration – Partner with product, design, data science, and adjacent engineering teams to build impactful features and solve complex business challenges.

 - Technical Leadership – Lead critical technical, design, and product discussions, driving best practices in system design, coding, testing, and DevOps.

 - Customer-Focused Innovation – Scope, build, and optimize products and services that deliver immediate and meaningful value to users.




REQUIREMENTS

 - 8+ years of experience designing, building, and scaling high-performance backend systems in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Expertise in architecting and productionizing large-scale distributed systems.

 - Comfortable working across multiple technical areas and collaborating with frontend/mobile teams.

 - Passion for tackling complex challenges with structured thinking and analytical rigor. Ability to break down intricate systems, identify flaws, and implement scalable, secure, and maintainable solutions.

 - Thrives in a fast-moving, iterative environment with a bias for action. Excited to take full ownership of projects, drive impact, and work cross-functionally with product, design, and engineering teams.

 - Strong understanding of data privacy, compliance, and secure coding practices. Experience with designing resilient and reliable systems that meet high security and regulatory standards.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with products related to lending, credit cards, payments, or other financial services.

 - Familiarity with mobile app ecosystems and financial technology


BENEFITS AND PERKS

Our goal is to provide a comprehensive offering of benefits and perks that promote better financial, mental, and physical wellness.



We believe working alongside each other in person is the best way to build a great product and foster a strong company culture. Our expectation is that employees are in the office five days a week, allowing for optimal collaboration, inclusivity, and productivity. At the same time, we understand that life happens and recognize the importance of flexibility. We are committed to supporting our employees when circumstances arise that require remote work or adjusted schedules. Our goal is to ensure everyone can effectively balance persona

source_jobs.title: Lifecycle Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY 

Credit Genie is a mobile-first financial wellness platform designed to help individuals take control of their financial future. We leverage artificial intelligence to provide personalized insights and are building a financial ecosystem by offering tools and services that provide instant access to cash, and building credit. Our goal is to empower every customer to achieve long-term financial stability.


Founded in 2019 by Ed Harycki https://www.linkedin.com/in/ed-harycki/, former Swift Capital Founder (acquired by PayPal in 2017 https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/10/paypal-acquires-swift-financial/). Backed by Khosla Ventures and led by industry pioneers from companies such as; PayPal, Square, and Cash App, we are well positioned to build the future of inclusive finance through cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.

Credit Genie is looking for a Senior Lifecycle Marketing Manager. A senior single domain owner to build, experiment with, and expand our lifecycle marketing strategy across loyalty, engagement, and retention. You’ll own the end-to-end customer lifecycle: every message, moment, and journey that turns a first-time user into a loyal, financially healthier customer.

We’re looking for someone who can think big and execute fast. You’ll be equally comfortable developing high-level strategy and rolling up your sleeves to write, build, test, and launch. If you’re energized by collaborating cross-functionally and driving measurable impact, this role is for you.

You’ll work closely with Product, Design, Research, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Brand to ensure our customer experience is consistent, cohesive, and deeply understood by customers.



What you’ll do:

 - Own lifecycle strategy end to end: Define and execute the strategy for engagement, retention, and loyalty across the full customer journey, from onboarding and activation to re-engagement, win-back, and long-term advocacy. Ensuring alignment with overall business objectives and customer needs.

 - Build and scale CRM programs: Design, build, and optimize cross-channel lifecycle programs across email, push, SMS, and in-app messaging using advanced segmentation, personalization, triggers, and automation. Move customers to the right action at the right moment, at scale.

 - Drive retention and reduce churn: Develop the segmentation, targeting, and journey logic that improves activation, deepens engagement, reduces churn, and grows lifetime value. Build proactive save, win-back, and re-engagement journeys grounded in customer behavior.

 - Design loyalty and engagement: Shape how Credit Genie rewards, recognizes, and re-engages customers over time. Build the loyalty and engagement frameworks that turn repeat usage into durable habits and lasting financial-wellness relationships.

 - Own messaging and lifecycle narrative: Craft clear, compelling lifecycle messaging and value propositions that ladder up to our broader brand strategy. Build scalable frameworks for consistent storytelling across every lifecycle touchpoint.

 - Run a test-and-learn engine: Build a rigorous experimentation practice, A/B and multivariate testing, cohort analysis, and holdouts to continuously improve performance. Define success metrics, post-launch evaluation, and iteration plans for every program.

 - Champion customer and market insights: Partner with Research and Data Science to turn user insights and behavioral trends into actionable lifecycle strategies that shape both marketing execution and product direction.

 - Establish operating rigor: Build the repeatable processes, playbooks, and launch frameworks that scale as the company grows, so lifecycle becomes a durable, measurable growth engine rather than a series of one-off campaigns.

 - Run a tight operation: Own the day-to-day health and quality of the program — email deliverability and IP reputation, push opt-in rates, and message frequency and send discipline. Maintain QA standards, launch checklis

notes: Mobile-first financial wellness platform with AI-driven insights
